AWS.name: Amazon RDS
AWS.description: Managed relational database. PostgreSQL and MySQL supported. Multi-AZ available.

fit.label: Recommended for this application
fit.appContext: Payments API uses PostgreSQL-compatible queries and requires transactional integrity for payment processing.
fit.basis: Based on current application pattern: API + relational data

detail.bestFor: Transactional workloads requiring ACID compliance, relational data models, and structured queries.
detail.avoidIf: You need schema-less storage or require sub-millisecond key-value access.
detail.governanceExplanation: Pre-approved for all ACME applications. No additional review required.
impactNotes: Standard for transactional workloads in ACME
Automated backups and multi-AZ supported
Integrates with existing VPC configuration

AWS.name: DynamoDB
AWS.description: Serverless NoSQL. Unlimited scale. Pay per request or provisioned capacity.

fit.label: Alternative available
fit.appContext: DynamoDB can handle session state or catalog data, but Payments API's relational queries are better served by RDS.
fit.basis: Based on workload type: Payments API uses structured relational queries rather than key-value patterns

detail.bestFor: Applications needing flexible schemas, massive scale, or event-driven access patterns with key-value or document models.
detail.avoidIf: Your workload requires complex joins, relational integrity, or multi-table transactions.
detail.governanceExplanation: Pre-approved for all ACME applications. No additional review required.
impactNotes: Suitable for session storage or catalog lookups
May require data model changes from relational schema
Consider if schema flexibility is a priority

AWS.name: ElastiCache
AWS.description: In-memory cache. Redis 7.x. Reduces database load for repeated queries.

fit.label: Useful for read-heavy traffic
fit.appContext: Can reduce Payments API database load for session data and repeated lookups, but requires approval.
fit.basis: Based on application read pattern: repeated lookups on low-churn data could benefit from caching layer

detail.bestFor: Applications with high read-to-write ratios where repeated queries can be cached.
detail.avoidIf: Your workload is write-heavy or requires strong consistency on every read.
detail.governanceExplanation: Requires platform team approval. Typical turnaround 1–2 business days.
impactNotes: Approval required before provisioning
Can significantly reduce RDS query load
Monitor cache invalidation strategy carefully

AWS.name: SQS
AWS.description: Managed message queuing. Standard and FIFO queues. Dead-letter queue support.

fit.label: Suitable for decoupled processing
fit.appContext: Payments API can use SQS to decouple async payment confirmation and notification workflows.
fit.basis: Based on application architecture: event-driven async operations benefit from a managed queue

detail.bestFor: Decoupling producers and consumers, handling async workflows, and buffering spikes in request load.
detail.avoidIf: You need synchronous request-response patterns or real-time streaming.
detail.governanceExplanation: Pre-approved for all ACME applications. No additional review required.
impactNotes: Low-cost entry point for async processing
FIFO queues available for ordered delivery
Pairs well with Lambda for event-driven patterns

AWS.name: Redshift
AWS.description: Analytics data warehouse. Columnar storage optimized for large-scale queries.

fit.label: Not recommended for this workload
fit.appContext: Payments API is a transactional application. Redshift is not suited to this workload type. Consider RDS.
fit.basis: Based on workload type: transactional workload rather than analytical query patterns

detail.bestFor: Large-scale analytical workloads, business intelligence, and complex reporting across massive datasets.
detail.avoidIf: Your workload is transactional, real-time, or does not involve analytical query patterns.
detail.governanceExplanation: Discouraged for this application type. Provisioning will require exception documentation and additional review.
impactNotes: Not suited to transactional workloads
Significantly higher cost than alternatives
Amazon RDS is the recommended alternative

AWS.name: Lambda
AWS.description: Serverless compute. Event-driven execution. Pay per invocation only.

fit.label: Suitable for async processing
fit.appContext: Lambda can handle async payment webhooks and event-driven side effects in Payments API.
fit.basis: Based on application architecture: serverless compute fits event-driven async handlers

detail.bestFor: Event-driven processing, lightweight async handlers, and short-lived compute tasks.
detail.avoidIf: You need persistent long-running processes, stateful compute, or high-memory workloads.
detail.governanceExplanation: Pre-approved for all ACME applications. No additional review required.
impactNotes: Pay-per-invocation keeps baseline cost near zero
Integrates natively with SQS, SNS, and API Gateway
Cold start latency may affect time-sensitive handlers

GCP.name: BigQuery
GCP.description: Serverless analytics warehouse. Scales to petabytes. Pay-per-query model.

fit.label: Recommended for this application
fit.appContext: Forecast Engine runs large analytical queries on historical data, which is BigQuery's core use case.
fit.basis: Based on current application pattern: batch analytics + large-scale query workload

detail.bestFor: Large-scale analytical queries, business intelligence, and batch data processing across massive datasets.
detail.avoidIf: Your workload is transactional or requires sub-second response on small lookups.
detail.governanceExplanation: Pre-approved for all ACME applications. No additional review required.
impactNotes: Standard analytics platform in ACME
Pay-per-query keeps costs predictable
Integrates with Looker and existing BI tooling

GCP.name: Cloud Storage
GCP.description: Object storage for any amount of data. Durable, available, and scalable.

fit.label: Suitable for model artifacts
fit.appContext: Forecast Engine can store trained model artifacts and raw input datasets in Cloud Storage.
fit.basis: Based on application workload: ML pipelines benefit from a durable object store for model checkpoints

detail.bestFor: Storing unstructured data, model artifacts, backups, and large binary objects at low cost.
detail.avoidIf: You need a structured database or low-latency key-value access.
detail.governanceExplanation: Pre-approved for all ACME applications. No additional review required.
impactNotes: Standard object store for ACME GCP workloads
Lifecycle policies available to reduce storage costs
Integrates with BigQuery for external table queries

GCP.name: Memorystore
GCP.description: Managed Redis and Memcached. Reduces latency for repeated query results.

fit.label: Useful for repeated query acceleration
fit.appContext: Forecast Engine runs repeated feature lookups that could be cached to reduce BigQuery scan costs.
fit.basis: Based on query pattern: frequently re-queried feature data is a good candidate for caching

detail.bestFor: Caching frequently accessed data to reduce backend load and query latency.
detail.avoidIf: Your data changes on every request or you require strong consistency guarantees.
detail.governanceExplanation: Requires platform team approval. Typical turnaround 1–2 business days.
impactNotes: Approval required before provisioning
Can reduce BigQuery scan costs on repeated queries
Plan cache invalidation strategy before provisioning

GCP.name: Cloud SQL
GCP.description: Managed PostgreSQL and MySQL. Fully managed with automated backups.

fit.label: Alternative available
fit.appContext: Cloud SQL can serve relational metadata storage for Forecast Engine, though BigQuery covers most analytical needs.
fit.basis: Based on workload type: metadata and config storage may benefit from a relational database alongside BigQuery

detail.bestFor: Structured relational data, configuration metadata, and transactional workloads alongside analytical pipelines.
detail.avoidIf: Your primary workload is analytical at scale — BigQuery is better suited for that.
detail.governanceExplanation: Pre-approved for all ACME applications. No additional review required.
impactNotes: Good fit for relational metadata alongside BigQuery
Automated backups and failover supported
Consider whether BigQuery alone covers your needs first
